one_time_description: Transfer to deferred revenue related to CMHC and ISC funding received but not yet recognized as revenue
economic_notes: Band receives significant funding through Comprehensive Funding Arrangement with ISC. Also receives major funding from First Nations Health Authority. COVID-19 noted as potential operational risk.
notes: Fiscal year is March 31, 2022 (not 2024). One subsidiary fully consolidated: Gitwangak Education Society. No GBEs. Large CMHC receipt of $4,129,294 in 2022 vs $23,653 prior year related to housing construction (secondary school $1,490,324 and child care facility $456,245 added as capital assets). Transfer to deferred revenue of $4,961,463 significantly reduces annual surplus from $9,003,726 to $4,042,263. Trust funds of $441,908 held by federal government not included in cash. No schedule of remuneration disclosed. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $7,370,901 + FNHA $1,566,529 + Province of BC $1,447,184 + FNESC $578,802 + CMHC $4,129,294 + BC Aboriginal Childcare Society $77,626 + New Relationship Trust $33,888 + First Peoples Heritage Fund $133,314 + Tricorp $83,426 = $15,420,964; OSR = property tax $30,849 + rental revenue $45,063 + Gitwangak Indian Band $3,000 + other revenue $1,072,343 = $1,151,255.
